Rotator Cuff Tear Physical Therapy

The rotator cuff is essential for shoulder stability and movement. When torn, it can cause pain, weakness, and limited function — but the right physical therapy can help you recover without surgery or prepare you for the best possible surgical outcome.

Call (985) 641-5825 Request Appointment

What Is the Rotator Cuff?

The rotator cuff is a group of four muscles and tendons that stabilize

146893c6cb69.plainText: What Is the Rotator Cuff? The rotator cuff is a group of four muscles and tendons that stabilize your shoulder and help you lift and rotate your arm. These tendons keep the upper arm bone securely in the shoulder socket.
146893c6cb69.originalHtml:

What Is the Rotator Cuff?

The rotator cuff is a group of four muscles and tendons that stabilize your shoulder and help you lift and rotate your arm. These tendons keep the upper arm bone securely in the shoulder socket.

What Is a Rotator Cuff Tear?

A rotator cuff tear occurs whe

746b81791f48.plainText: What Is a Rotator Cuff Tear? A rotator cuff tear occurs when one or more of these tendons become damaged or torn. This can happen suddenly (acute injury) or develop over time (degenerative tear). Common causes include: ✓ Lifting something too heavy ✓ Repetitive overhead movements ✓ Falls or sudden injuries ✓ Age-related wear and tear
746b81791f48.originalHtml:
What Is a Rotator Cuff Tear?

A rotator cuff tear occurs when one or more of these tendons become damaged or torn. This can happen suddenly (acute injury) or develop over time (degenerative tear).

Common causes include:

  • ✓ Lifting something too heavy
  • ✓ Repetitive overhead movements
  • ✓ Falls or sudden injuries
  • ✓ Age-related wear and tear

is a group of four muscles and tendons that stabilize your shoulder and help you lift and rotate your arm. These tendons keep the upper arm bone securely in the shoulder socket.

What Is a Rotator Cuff Tear?

A rotator cuff tear occurs when one or more of these tendons become damaged or torn. This can happen suddenly (acute injury) or develop over time (degenerative tear).

Common causes include:

  • ✓ Lifting something too heavy
  • ✓ Repetitive overhead movements
  • ✓ Falls or sudden injuries
  • ✓ Age-related wear and tear

Signs and Symptoms

708ad97dadc7.plainText: Signs and Symptoms ▸ Shoulder pain, especially when lifting or lowering your arm ▸ Weakness in the shoulder ▸ Difficulty reaching overhead or behind your back ▸ Pain that worsens at night or when lying on the affected side
708ad97dadc7.originalHtml:
Signs and Symptoms

  • ▸ Weakness in the shoulder
  • ▸ Difficulty reaching overhead or behind your back
  • ▸ Pain that worsens at night or when lying on the affected side
